response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which makes his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David gathered three times as many as Sophia implying Sophia gathered 13 blueberries. Together they collected 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. This equals 76 blueberries in total. 

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins needed 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make divide the total number of blueberries by the number needed per batch. 76 divided by 9 gives 8 full batches with 4 blueberries left over. 

***

Each batch was sold for $5. To calculate the total money made multiply the number of batches by the price per batch. 8 times 5 gives 40 dollars. The leftover blueberries were not enough to make another batch so they did not earn money from them. 

***

The total amount of money they made from selling the muffins was 40 dollars. This was the result of their combined efforts in gathering and using the blueberries. Their work brought in a significant amount of money for their youth.
